title: agrostis crinum-ursi
intro: Agrostis crinum-ursi, also known as Bearberry or Uva-ursi, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to North America and Europe, characterized by its small white flowers and red berries. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating urinary tract infections and inflammation due to its high content of arbutin, a natural antibacterial compound. The health benefits of Agrostis crinum-ursi include its ability to act as a diuretic, reducing urine acidity and alleviating symptoms associated with urinary tract infections, as well as its potential to support kidney function and reduce inflammation. Agrostis crinum-ursi can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, with the most common herbal preparation being a dried leaf extract that can be consumed as a supplement or added to herbal remedies for its medicinal properties.
